settings.centerDescription: Keep translation work focused in the workspace and move provider, model, execution, and trust-boundary decisions into one durable place.

general.title: General settings
general.description: Choose the default request path for translation tasks and keep system-wide behavior consistent across providers.
general.providerOverrideTitle: Provider overrides still live with the provider
general.providerOverrideDescription: Execution mode can be overridden per provider inside the edit flow, so global defaults stay predictable without blocking edge cases.
general.workspaceScopeTitle: Current-task controls stay in the translator
general.workspaceScopeDescription: Language selection and in-flight model decisions stay in the main translation workspace. This page only covers durable workspace defaults.

languages.title: Languages and translation preferences
languages.description: Keep language-aware defaults understandable without moving task-time choices away from the translation workspace.
languages.currentTaskTitle: Current translation targets stay on the main page
languages.currentTaskDescription: Target languages for the task you are actively translating are still chosen in the translation workspace where the task context is visible.
languages.defaultsTitle: Global default target languages are not part of this release
languages.defaultsDescription: This settings center does not introduce a new persisted default target-language field. It keeps the architecture honest instead of showing fake settings.
languages.bindingTitle: Language-to-model bindings belong to model management
languages.bindingDescription: If a language should consistently prefer a specific model set, configure it in Provider model management where available models and providers are visible together.

providers.title: Providers
providers.description: Manage credentials, base URLs, protocol mode, import/export, and the provider catalog that powers the whole translation workspace.
providers.listDescription: Providers and their models are treated as one continuous configuration asset. Edit providers here, then refine model availability in the model page.
providers.emptyDescription: Add a provider first so the workspace can discover models, set defaults, and execute translations.
providers.emptySearchDescription: Try another provider or model keyword, or clear the search to review the full provider inventory.

models.title: Models
models.description: Control which models are available, which model is the workspace default, and how specific languages bind to model sets.
models.listDescription: The model page stays flat for fast scanning, while still preserving the provider relationship on every row.
models.emptyTitle: No providers yet, so there are no models to manage
models.emptyDescription: Create a provider first. Once a provider exists, you can enable models, set a default, and create language-specific bindings here.
models.emptySearchDescription: Try another model name, provider name, or clear the search to see the full model inventory.
models.bindingDescription: Choose a language to review or override its model set. If you leave a language empty, translation falls back to the global default model.
models.bindingSummaryCustom: {{count}} model binding(s) selected for this language.
models.bindingSummaryFallback: No custom binding is set. This language will use the global default model.
models.bindingHint: Bindings here affect future translation tasks across the workspace. Use the language-tag popover in the translator for one-off task-time overrides.

about.title: About and security notes
about.description: This product keeps credentials in the browser for user-managed configuration flows. These notes explain what that means in practice.
about.localStorageTitle: Browser storage is convenient, not enterprise secret storage
about.localStorageDescription: Provider credentials are currently stored on the browser side for user-managed settings flows. Treat the browser environment as part of your trust boundary.
about.exportDescription: Exported configuration files can include provider credentials, model lists, and default behavior. Handle them like secrets and avoid sharing them casually.
about.browserDirectTitle: Browser direct keeps requests in the browser context
about.browserDirectDescription: When browser direct mode is used, API keys and request traffic remain in the browser execution context and go straight to the upstream provider endpoint.
about.serverProxyTitle: Server proxy changes the request path, not the overall trust model
about.serverProxyDescription: Server proxy sends requests through the app server before forwarding upstream, but deployment operators remain inside the trust boundary for the overall system.
